description: Alerting rules for the GCP Security Finding Chain scenario (SCC, SecOps)

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP Security Finding Chain — SCC HIGH/CRITICAL
rules.investigationGuide:
# SCC HIGH/CRITICAL Findings

Security Command Center raised a HIGH or CRITICAL finding.

## Triage
1. List findings: `FROM logs-gcp.scc-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND gcp.scc.severity IN ("HIGH", "CRITICAL") | KEEP @timestamp, gcp.scc.severity, gcp.scc.category, source.ip | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 25`
2. Identify the resource (project, asset, or principal)
3. If exfiltration class → see Data Exfil runbook; if IAM abuse → see IAM runbook

## Escalate when
- **Always page security on-call** for HIGH/CRITICAL
- Customer data or secrets implicated

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP Security Finding Chain — Multi-Stage Chain Burst
rules.investigationGuide:
# Multi-Stage Chain Burst

Multiple alert-class docs across SCC, SecOps, or related stores in a short window.

## Triage
1. Source breakdown: `FROM logs-gcp.scc-*, logs-gcp.secops-*, logs-gcp.securitylake-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 30m AND event.kind == "alert" | STATS findings = COUNT(*) BY event.dataset | SORT findings DESC`
2. One source spiking = less interesting; three = investigate
3. Check for shared source.ip or user.name

## Escalate when
- Spike crosses three or more sources with shared pivot
- Includes HIGH/CRITICAL severity findings

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP Security Finding Chain — SecOps Case Created
rules.investigationGuide:
# SecOps Case Created

A SecOps case was opened — validate scope and SCC posture around the same window.

## Triage
1. Open the case in SecOps: confirm priority, assignee, and linked alerts
2. SCC compliance drift: `FROM logs-gcp.scc-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND gcp.scc.compliance.status == "FAILED" | STATS failures = COUNT(*) BY gcp.scc.compliance.standard | SORT failures DESC | LIMIT 25`
3. Check Cloud Audit Logs for recent IAM or resource changes tied to the case

## Escalate when
- Case references regulated data (PCI, HIPAA, SOC 2) or critical assets
- Same resource fails multiple SCC compliance checks

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP Security Finding Chain — SecOps High Match Volume
rules.investigationGuide:
# Multiple SCC Alerts With Source IP

Burst of findings with external source IPs — likely external scanner or attacker.

## Triage
1. Top IPs: `FROM logs-gcp.scc-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.kind == "alert" AND source.ip IS NOT NULL | STATS findings = COUNT(*) BY source.ip | SORT findings DESC | LIMIT 10`
2. One IP = targeted; many IPs = broader campaign
3. Check threat intel feeds for the IP

## Escalate when
- Source IP on known threat feed
- Targets include customer-facing resources
